info.name: Just Book IT — Mobile API
info.description:
Customer (v1), Vendor (v2), and Driver (v3) APIs.

**Base URL:** {{base_url}}

**v1 flow:** OTP login → Home → Catalog → Cart → Checkout → Payment (or single-item Booking → Payment)
**v2 flow:** Config → OTP login → Home → Bookings / Products / Portfolio Gallery / Chats / Profile

**Products** = items the vendor wants to sell or rent (admin approval). **Portfolio** = vendor's previous work photos (no approval).

**OTP:** data.otp in Send OTP response.
**v1 IDs:** v1_booking_id, v1_checkout_order_id, v1_cart_item_id, v1_chat_id, v1_product_id, v1_designer_id
**v2 IDs:** v2_booking_id, v2_product_id, v2_chat_id

**v1 highlights:** Designer profile with reviews & full address. Catalog double-filter by audience + service. Booking cancel requires reason. Delivered bookings can be reviewed. Location dropdowns at `/v1/locations/*`. Notifications at `/v1/notifications` with read/unread. Home returns `notifications.unread_count`.

**v2 highlights:** Notifications at `/v2/notifications` with read/unread. Home returns `notifications.unread_count`. Location dropdowns at `/v2/locations/*` (countries, states, cities).

**v3 flow:** OTP login → Dashboard → Deliveries (accept/pickup/deliver) → Payments → Profile
**v3 IDs:** v3_delivery_id

**v3 highlights:** Dashboard stats + new deliveries. Delivery tabs: new | accepted | out_for_delivery | completed | cancelled. Complete delivery with customer OTP. Wallet + withdraw.

request.description: Runs migrate --force, optimize:clear, cache:clear, config:clear, view:clear, route:clear and clears storage/framework cache + compiled views. No auth required.

Public endpoint. Bearer token optional. `?city=` filters featured designers by vendor city; when logged in without `city`, uses the customer profile city. Includes notifications when Bearer token is sent.
List sub-categories under a main category (Women/Men/Kids). Use returned id as `subcategory_id` when creating products or filtering catalog.
Nested country → state → city tree for offline dropdowns.

query.description: Shop category: women | men | kids (aliases: shop_category, shop_category_id)
Service: fashion-designer | rented-dress | rented-jewellery (aliases: rental-cloth, service_id)
Optional — filter catalog to a specific vendor
Optional — sub-category slug (e.g. women-sarees). Alias: subcategory_id
Optional — filter by vendor city (exact match, case-insensitive)

request.description: Catalog list with filters: audience, service, subcategory slug, vendor_id, city (vendor city). Response includes filters.cities list. Each item includes `is_available`.

query.description: Short slug resolves to women-sarees when audience is set

request.description: Rental dress catalog filtered by subcategory slug. Each item includes `is_available`.
Product detail with `is_available`, `review_summary`, and reviews (each review includes `profile_image_url`).

Designer profile with `cover_image_url`, `coverImage`, `is_available`, `contact` (mobile, email, business_mobile, business_email), address, reviews (with customer `profile_image_url`), and portfolio/products.
Returns cart summary (per-vendor subtotals and delivery fees) and line items. Multi-vendor cart is supported.
Add product to cart. Returns 422 if the product is already in the cart. Multiple vendors allowed in one cart.
Remove one item from cart by cart item id.

item.name: Checkout (multi-vendor)
item.description: Cart → checkout preview → create parent checkout_order → pay once → sub-orders per vendor (JB######-V1, V2…). Measurements stored once on parent. Partial refund auto on vendor reject.

request.description: Price rollup from cart with per-vendor delivery fee via vendor_shipments.

body.raw:
{
  "delivery_address": "12 MG Road, Koramangala, Bengaluru",
  "city": "Bengaluru",
  "pincode": "560034",

  "rental_end_date": "2026-07-03",

  "measure_chest_cm": 88,
  "measure_waist_cm": 70,
  "vendor_shipments": [
    { "vendor_id": 1, "shipment_required": true }
  ]
}

request.description: Creates checkout_orders parent + sub-orders per vendor. Clears cart. Measurements saved once on parent.
Parent order with nested sub_orders, measurements, refunds and refund_histories.
Checkout preview for a product. Includes cart summary/items when the customer has cart entries, plus cart_item_status for the previewed product (in_cart, message when already added).

formdata.description: Optional — max 5, JPG/PNG/WebP, 4MB each

query.description: fashion_designer | rental_dress | rental_jewellery (aliases: designers, fashion-designer, rental, jewellery)

request.description: Full booking detail. When status is `in_progress`, response includes `delivery_otp` (4-digit code for delivery confirmation).
Cancel a booking (new or pending_acceptance only). `reason` is required (5–500 chars).
Submit a review after booking is delivered. One review per booking. Updates vendor rating.
Requires `enable_cod` in admin platform settings.
Payment summary for multi-vendor checkout parent order.
Single payment marks all sub-orders paid and moves them to pending_acceptance.
Broadcast notifications for customers. Each item includes `is_read`, `read_at`. Response includes `summary.unread_count`. Filter: all | read | unread.

{
  "label": "Home",

request.description: Same fields as Add Measurement. Send any or all keys — each provided field updates individually.

item.name: v2 — Vendor App
item.description:
Vendor mobile app APIs. Flow: Config → Send OTP → Verify OTP → Home → Bookings / Products / Chats / Profile.

Bearer token saved as `v2_token`. IDs auto-saved: `v2_booking_id`, `v2_product_id`, `v2_chat_id`.

**Profile:** `coverImage` on `/v2/auth/me` and `POST /v2/auth/profile`. Availability: `POST /v2/profile/available`, `POST /v2/profile/unavailable`, or `POST /v2/profile/availability` with `is_available`.

**Products:** filter by service type — `?type=rented-dress`, `?type=fashion-designer`, `?type=rented-jewellery`. Each item includes `is_available`.

**Booking tabs:** `accepted`, `in_progress` (In Transit), `completed` (Delivered), `cancelled`, `returned`, `rework`, `re-in-transit`, `re-delivered`, `new`.

**Status update** accepts aliases: `in_progress`, `completed`, `re-intransit`, `re-delivered`. Response uses `status` (alias) + `status_raw` (DB).

**Returned bookings:** `POST /v2/bookings/{id}/damage` with `damage_note` and `damage_deduct_percent`.

**Booking detail:** customer email/mobile, measurements, payment_summary, `customer_review` when delivered.

request.description: Returns vendor profile including `coverImage` / `cover_image_url`, `bio`, `is_available`, shop logo, and business details.

formdata.value: Fashion designer specialising in bridal and evening wear.

formdata.description: 1 = available, 0 = unavailable (also via POST /v2/profile/availability)
Profile photo
Shop banner / background image (alias: cover_image). Single image only.

request.description: Update vendor profile. Supports `coverImage` (or `cover_image`) for shop banner, `profile_image`, `bio`, and `is_available`. Same fields returned on GET /v2/auth/me.
Vendor app config including `shop_categories`, `subcategories`, product types, sizes, and locations.

Default product catalog for vendor apps. Returns `categories` (Men/Women/Kids with nested `subcategories`) and `service_categories` (Rented Dress, Fashion Designer, etc.).

**Service filter:** `?service=rented-jewellery` returns only jewellery sub-categories. `?service=rented-dress` returns dress sub-categories. `?service=fashion-designer` returns the **same dress sub-categories** as rented-dress (plus any fashion-designer-specific ones). Invalid or missing `service` returns all data.

Also accepts `service_category_id` or `service_id`. Response includes `applied` when a service filter is active.

Use `?include_subcategories=0` for main categories only.
Sub-categories for one main category. Use when the app loads sub-categories on demand after category selection.
Legacy combined response: `categories` with nested sub-categories and `services` list.
Legacy flat filter. Prefer GET /v2/categories/{id}/subcategories.
Public. Use for vendor registration / business address dropdowns.
Full nested country → state → city tree.
Vendor dashboard. `notifications` includes `unread_count`, `total_count`, `unread_chats`, and `new_bookings`.
Broadcast notifications for vendors. Each item includes `is_read`, `read_at`. Filter: all | read | unread.
Each item includes `rented_period`, `rented_period_label`, `rental_period`, and date labels for the rental window.
Delivered bookings — use Booking Detail to see `customer_review`. Status in response shows as `completed`.

Full booking detail at `data.*` — includes `order_items` / `line_items` (per-line status, `can_accept` / `can_reject`, size/color), `checkout`, `items_count`, `pending_items_count`, plus `customer`, `product`, `payment_summary`, tracking fields, etc. Saves first line item id to `v2_order_item_id`.
Accept entire vendor sub-order (all pending `order_items`). Response includes `order_items` / `line_items` with per-item status.
Reject entire vendor sub-order. `reason` is required (5–500 characters). Cancels all items and may create a partial refund for checkout orders.
Accept a single line item (`order_items[].id` from booking detail). Booking stays `pending_acceptance` until all remaining items are accepted or rejected.
Reject a single line item. `reason` required. Recalculates sub-order totals and may issue a line-item partial refund. If all items are rejected, booking becomes cancelled.
In Transit tab — sends `in_progress`. Generates delivery_otp and pickup_address.
Delivered tab — alias `completed` maps to DB status `delivered`.
Rental flow — after delivered, mark returned then use Update Damage.
Designer flow: delivered → rework → re-intransit → re-delivered. Also accepts aliases `re-intransit` and `re-delivered`.
Record damage deduction when booking status is `returned`.
Valid transitions: accepted→in_progress→completed (delivered)→returned/rework→re-intransit→re-delivered.

item.name: Products
item.description:
Items the vendor wants to sell or rent (dresses, jewellery, designer services). Stored as `portfolio_items`. New products are **pending** until approved in admin **Products** (Content menu).

**Service types** (separate lists): `type=rented-dress`, `type=fashion-designer`, `type=rented-jewellery`.

Each product includes `is_available` (approved + vendor active). List response also includes `vendor_is_available`.

Product detail includes `reviews` and `review_summary`.

Not the same as **Portfolio** — the vendor's previous work (`/v2/portfolio`).

request.description: Rental dress products. Response includes `vendor_is_available` and per-item `is_available`, `category_type`, variants, damage rules.
Fashion designer service products (separate from rental dresses).

Product detail with `gallery_image_urls` (images + videos in one array), plus optional `video_urls` / `gallery_videos`, variants, damage_deductions, `is_available`, `is_listing_active`, `availability_status`, `reviews`, and `review_summary`.

formdata.description: Product tab: rented-dress | rented-jewellery | fashion-designer

formdata.value: Premium velvet evening gown for rent.

formdata.description: Product price per day (required)
Optional advance/booking amount
Required. Sub-category id from GET /v1/categories?type=sub&parent_id={main_category_id}
Optional — derived from sub-category parent when subcategory_id is set
Primary product image (required if no gallery_images)
Extra product images (add multiple rows with same key)
Product videos (mp4/mov/avi/mkv/webm/3gp/mpeg/mpg/m4v/wmv/flv/ogv). Add multiple rows with same key. Max 5, 100MB each.
JSON array — size, color, price per variant (no image in JSON when using variant_images)
Variant image for index 0 (matches variants[0]). Add another row with the same key for variants[1], etc.
Variant image for index 1 (optional — add one row per variant index)
JSON array of damage rules (damage_type, percent)

request.description: Create product for a service type. Set `type` to rented-dress, fashion-designer, or rented-jewellery. Requires `subcategory_id` and primary `image` (or at least one image in `gallery_images`). Send multiple `gallery_images` with both images and videos in the same field. Optional separate `videos` / `gallery_videos` keys still work. Send `variants` as JSON (size, color, price). Upload variant photos index-wise via `variant_images`.

formdata.value: Bespoke bridal lehenga design and stitching.

formdata.description: Service price (no /day label for fashion-designer)
Optional — change service type: rented-dress | fashion-designer | rented-jewellery
women | men | kids
Optional — replace primary image
Optional — append gallery images
Optional — append product videos (all common formats)
JSON array — replaces all variants (size, color, price). Use variant_images for photos by index.
Variant image for index 0. Add more rows with the same key for index 1, 2, …
Replaces all damage rules when sent

request.description: Update product — same fields as create (title, description, price, advance, audience, type, image, gallery, variants, damage_deductions). Resets status to pending for re-approval.
Mark an approved product as available for customers. No body required. Sets `is_listing_active=true`.
Mark an approved product as unavailable. No body required. Sets `is_listing_active=false`.
Set product availability with boolean body `is_available` (true/false). Only approved products can be toggled.

body.raw:
{
  "shop_name": "Riya Rathore Studio",
  "service_types": ["Fashion Designer", "Rental Dresses"],
  "business_mobile": "9876543211",
  "business_mail": "business@riyastudio.com",
  "address": "123 MG Road, Indore"
}

formdata.value: Fashion designer specialising in bridal and evening wear.

request.description: Legacy bio + cover upload. Prefer POST /v2/auth/profile with `bio` and `coverImage` fields.
Set availability with boolean body. Prefer POST /v2/profile/available or /unavailable for one-tap toggles.
Mark vendor shop as available. No request body. Sets is_listing_active = true.
Mark vendor shop as unavailable. No request body. Sets is_listing_active = false.

item.name: Portfolio Gallery
item.description:
The vendor's **previous work** — showcase photos (women / men / kids). Stored in `vendor_portfolio_images`. Managed by the vendor in profile settings. **No admin approval**.

For items they want to sell or rent, use **Products** (`/v2/products`) instead.

item.name: v3 — Driver
item.description:
Driver mobile app APIs. Flow: Config → Send OTP → Verify OTP → Dashboard → Deliveries → Payments / Notifications / Profile.

Bearer token saved as `v3_token`. IDs auto-saved: `v3_delivery_id`, `v3_notification_id`.

**Dashboard** (`GET /v3/home`): stats, earnings (month/ytd), COD collected, `cash_collected_orders` (full COD order details), recent deliveries. Filters: `month`, `year`, `from`, `to`, `date`, `search`. List items include `customer_image_url` / `imageUrl`.

**Delivery tabs:** `new`, `accepted`, `pickup`, `dispatched`, `delivered`, `cancelled`, `rescheduled`.

**Actions:** accept → pickup → dispatch → delivered (OTP + optional `delivery_image`). Rescheduled deliveries can dispatch directly and complete with OTP. Reject requires `reason`. Reschedule requires `scheduled_date`.

**Payments** (`GET /v3/payments`): wallet transactions with `customer_image_url` / `imageUrl`; returns sample demo rows when wallet is empty (`is_sample_data: true`).

**Notifications** (`GET /v3/notifications`): broadcast inbox with `is_read`, filter `all|read|unread`. Mark one read: `POST /v3/notifications/{id}/read`. Mark all read: `POST /v3/notifications/read-all`.

request.description: Returns delivery_tabs: new, accepted, pickup, dispatched, delivered, cancelled, rescheduled.

Delivery dashboard: stats, earnings (this_month, ytd), cod.total_collected + orders_count, cash_collected_orders (full delivery detail for COD collected on filtered date), recent_deliveries with customer_image_url/imageUrl and payment_badge.
New unassigned deliveries. Optional filters: `from`, `to`, `date`, `search`.
Reject/skip delivery. `reason` is required (3–500 chars). Works on New (unassigned) or Accepted/Rescheduled.
Mark out for delivery after pickup. Alias: POST /out-for-delivery

Complete delivery with customer OTP. Rescheduled deliveries can also be completed directly with OTP. Optional proof photo: use form-data with `delivery_image` file. Alias: POST /deliver. COD orders set cod_collected_at automatically.

formdata.description: Optional delivery proof photo

request.description: Same as Complete Delivery but multipart/form-data. `delivery_image` is optional.
Use OTP from GET /v1/bookings/{id} when order is in_progress.
Reschedule an accepted/pickup/dispatched delivery. `scheduled_date` required (today or later).
Wallet transaction history with search/date filters. Each item includes `customer_image_url` and `imageUrl`. When no transactions exist, returns sample demo rows and `is_sample_data: true`.
Broadcast notifications for drivers. Each item includes `is_read`, `read_at`. Filter: all | read | unread. Response includes `summary.unread_count`.

item.name: E2E — Full Order Flow
item.description:
Run these in order to test Customer → Vendor → Driver flow.

**Setup:** `php artisan db:seed --class=OrderFlowDemoSeeder` then `php artisan test:order-flow`

**Status chain:** new → pay (COD) → pending_acceptance → vendor accept → in_progress → driver accept → pickup → dispatch → delivered

**Driver demo mobile:** 9898989898 (active, pre-seeded)

request.description:
On server run: php artisan test:order-flow

This validates the entire chain automatically.
Generates delivery_otp and pickup_address automatically.
Copy data.delivery_otp for driver deliver step.
Replace 1234 with OTP from step 7. Alias: /deliver
